qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SSRF via Data Import URL due to disabled SSRF protection by default
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes SSRF through the data-import URL feature caused by SSRF_PROTECTION_ENABLED defaulting to False in core/utils/io.py. The advisory describes stored XSS via the custom_hotkeys field rendered unsafely in templates/base.html and users/api.py. The root causes, vulnerable code paths, and vulnerability classes are entirely different.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The advisory describes stored XSS via the custom_hotkeys field in templates/base.html (using the |safe filter without escaping </script> sequences) chained with IDOR in users/api.py for account takeover. The only qualifying finding (ssrf-via-import-url) describes a genuine SSRF in Label Studio's import URL feature but with a completely different root cause (SSRF_PROTECTION_ENABLED defaults to False in core/utils/io.py).
evidence: Advisory root cause: templates/base.html renders user.custom_hotkeys via |safe filter, core/templatetags/filters.py json_dumps_ensure_ascii does not escape </script>, users/api.py PATCH allows XSS payloads in custom_hotkeys.

ssrf-via-import-url root cause: SSRF_PROTECTION_ENABLED defaults to False in core/settings/base.py, causing validate_upload_url to skip private-IP checks in core/utils/io.py. Different vulnerable function, different code path, different vulnerability class.
